questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following does not apply to sound waves in fluids?
options: They travel faster in liquids than in gases
questions.solution:
The option that does not apply to sound waves in fluids is:

Option C

They are transverse

Explanation:

Sound waves in fluids (liquids and gases) are longitudinal waves, not transverse waves. In longitudinal waves, the oscillations of the particles of the medium are parallel to the direction of wave propagation. This means that particles in the medium move back and forth in the same direction that the wave is traveling.

Let's break down the other options to see why they do apply to sound waves in fluids:

Option A: They transport energy

Sound waves indeed transport energy. As a sound wave propagates through a medium, it transfers energy from one particle to another through the medium.

Option B: They need a medium to travel

Sound waves require a medium (solid, liquid, or gas) to travel. They cannot propagate in a vacuum because there are no particles to transmit the vibrations.

Option D: They travel faster in liquids than in gases

Sound waves generally travel faster in liquids than in gases. This is because particles in liquids are more closely packed than in gases, allowing the sound waves to transmit vibrations more efficiently.

Therefore, Option C is the correct answer since sound waves in fluids are not transverse waves.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The heating element in an electric iron is usually made of
questions.solution:
The heating element in an electric iron is typically made of a material that has a high resistance to generate sufficient heat when electric current passes through it. Among the options provided, the best choice for this application is Nichrome, which is a nickel-chromium alloy. Nichrome is preferred because it has a high melting point, excellent resistance to oxidation, and stable resistance over a wide range of temperatures.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C Nichrome

To elaborate more, when electric current flows through Nichrome, it encounters resistance, converting electrical energy into heat energy. This principle of Joule heating is utilized in various heating applications, such as in electric irons, toasters, and hair dryers. The equation governing this process is given by:

$$Q = I^2 R t$$

where:

  • $$Q$$ is the heat energy produced in joules
  • $$I$$ is the current in amperes
  • $$R$$ is the resistance in ohms
  • $$t$$ is the time in seconds

Using Nichrome allows manufacturers to create reliable and efficient heating elements that can withstand high temperatures and prolonged usage.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The flash of lightning is seen before the thunderstorm is heard. It verifies that
options: sound travels much faster than light
light travels much faster than sound
light and sound both travel with same speed
intensity of flash of lightning is very high during thunderstorm
questions.solution:
The phenomenon where the flash of lightning is seen before the thunderstorm is heard can be explained by the difference in the speeds at which light and sound travel. Light travels significantly faster than sound. Thus, the correct option is:

Option B: light travels much faster than sound

To elaborate, light travels at an approximate speed of $$3 \times 10^8 \, \text{m/s}$$ in a vacuum, and slightly slower in the atmosphere. On the other hand, sound travels at a much slower speed of approximately $$343 \, \text{m/s}$$ in air at room temperature. Because of this large discrepancy in speeds, the light from the lightning is observed almost instantaneously, while the sound waves take a longer time to reach the observer's ears. This delay is why we see the flash of lightning before we hear the thunder.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Why a fielder in the ground gradually pulls his hands backward while catching a fast moving cricket ball ?
options: To reduce the acceleration of the ball
To increase the acceleration of the ball
To increase the velocity of the ball
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 1: To reduce the acceleration of the ball.

Explanation:

When a fielder catches a fast-moving cricket ball, the ball has a large momentum. To stop it, the fielder has to bring its momentum to zero.

We know:

$$ \text{Force} = \frac{\Delta p}{\Delta t} $$

where:

  • $\Delta p$ = change in momentum

  • $\Delta t$ = time taken

The change in momentum is fixed because the ball has to be stopped. So, if the fielder pulls his hands backward, the time of stopping $\Delta t$ increases.

When time increases, the force decreases.

Also, since acceleration is related to force by

$$ F = ma $$

less force means less acceleration (actually less retardation).

So, by moving his hands backward, the fielder reduces the acceleration of the ball, and therefore the force on his hands becomes less. This prevents injury.

Final Answer:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 1: To reduce the acceleration of the ball}} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements with reference to L-Wave or Long Wave generated by an earthquake is/are correct?1.They follow the Earth's circumference
2.They travel at more or less at a constant rate
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Statement 1 is correct and Statement 2 is not.

L-waves (surface waves) travel along the Earth’s surface (i.e. its circumference) but are dispersive, so their speed varies with frequency.

Answer: 1 only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements best defines the concept of heat?
options: The transformation of energy from one form to another
The conversion of energy into mass and vice-versa due to temperature difference
The transfer of energy due to temperature difference
The change in volume of a substance with temperature
questions.solution:
Option 1: The transformation of energy from one form to another.

This describes how energy can change its form, like chemical energy turning into electrical energy in a battery, or potential energy turning into kinetic energy as something falls. While heat can be involved in these transformations, this statement describes energy conversion in general, not specifically what heat is.

Option 2: The conversion of energy into mass and vice-versa due to temperature difference.

This touches on the concept of mass-energy equivalence, often represented by Einstein's famous equation $$E=mc^2$$. However, this isn't how we define heat in everyday thermodynamics. Heat isn't about energy converting into mass.

Option 3: The transfer of energy due to temperature difference.

Bingo! This is the most accurate definition. Heat is energy that flows from a warmer object or region to a cooler one because there's a difference in their temperatures. Think about holding an ice cube – heat flows from your warmer hand to the colder ice cube, making your hand feel cold as it loses energy.

Option 4: The change in volume of a substance with temperature.

This phenomenon is called thermal expansion (or contraction), and it's a result of temperature changes. When a substance heats up, its particles move more vigorously and usually spread out, causing an increase in volume. While related to temperature, it's not the definition of heat itself.

So, the statement that best defines the concept of heat is Option 3: The transfer of energy due to temperature difference.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A mass is attached to a spring that hangs vertically. The extension produced in the spring is 6 cm on Earth. The acceleration due to gravity on the surface of the Moon is one-sixth of its value on the surface of the Earth. The extension of the spring on the Moon would be:
questions.solution:
A spring that obeys Hooke's Law extends by a distance proportional to the force applied to it. When a mass $ m $ is attached to a vertical spring, the force applied to the spring is the weight of the mass, which can be expressed as $ F = mg $, where $ g $ is the acceleration due to gravity.

Given that the extension produced in the spring on Earth is 6 cm (or 0.06 m), we can write this as:

$$ F_\text{Earth} = k \cdot \Delta x_\text{Earth} $$

where $ k $ is the spring constant and $ \Delta x_\text{Earth} $ is the extension on Earth. Rearranging this equation, we get:

$$ mg = k \cdot 0.06 $$

Since we are comparing the extension on the Moon to the Earth, we need to consider the change in the gravitational force. On the Moon, the acceleration due to gravity $ g_\text{Moon} $ is one-sixth of that on Earth:

$$ g_\text{Moon} = \frac{g}{6} $$

The force exerted by the mass on the Moon would then be:

$$ F_\text{Moon} = mg_\text{Moon} = m \cdot \frac{g}{6} $$

This force causes a different extension in the spring, which we will denote as $ \Delta x_\text{Moon} $. Applying Hooke’s Law on the Moon, we have:

$$ F_\text{Moon} = k \cdot \Delta x_\text{Moon} $$

Substituting for $ F_\text{Moon} $ from earlier, we get:

$$ m \cdot \frac{g}{6} = k \cdot \Delta x_\text{Moon} $$

Since we know from the Earth’s situation that $ mg = k \cdot 0.06 $, we can substitute $ mg $ into the equation:

$$ \frac{k \cdot 0.06}{6} = k \cdot \Delta x_\text{Moon} $$

Simplifying this, we find:

$$ \Delta x_\text{Moon} = \frac{0.06}{6} = 0.01 \, \text{m} $$

Therefore, the extension of the spring on the Moon would be 1 cm. So, the correct answer is:

Option B: 1 cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Sita, 1.5 m high, stands before a plane mirror fixed on a wall to view her full image. What should be the minimum height of the plane mirror so that Sita can view her image fully?
questions.solution:
To determine the minimum height of the plane mirror required for Sita to view her full image, we need to understand the properties of a plane mirror. A plane mirror reflects light in such a way that the image formed is virtual, upright, and of the same size as the object. In order for her to see her complete image, the length of the mirror must be half the height of Sita.

Here's why: When Sita looks at her reflection in the mirror, the topmost and bottommost rays from her body will reflect off the mirror at equal angles. To capture both her head and feet in the reflection, the mirror needs to be at least half her height. This is because each point on Sita's body is reflected in the corresponding point on the mirror at half the height, allowing the complete image to fit.

So, if Sita's height is 1.5 m, the minimum height of the mirror should be:

$$\frac{1.5 \, \text{m}}{2} = 0.75 \, \text{m}$$

Thus, the correct option is:

Option C: 0.75 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which of the following arrangement of resistors offers minimum effective resistance between points X and Y?
questions.solution:
(a)

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{R_e}=\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{3}=\frac{3}{3}=1 \\ & \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{e}}=1 \Omega \end{aligned} $$

(b) $\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{e}}=\frac{3 \times 3}{3+3}=\frac{9}{6}=\frac{3}{2}=1.5 \Omega$

(c) $\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{e}}=1+1=2 \Omega$

(d) $R_e=1+1+1=3 \Omega$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The length of a simple pendulum is increased four times to its previous value while the mass is doubled. What is the ratio of the new and previous time period of the pendulum?
questions.solution:
Calculation:

The time period of a simple pendulum is given by the formula:

$ T_{\text{old}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

If the length $ L $ of the pendulum is increased four times and the mass is doubled, we note that the time period is independent of the pendulum's mass. Therefore, the time period depends only on the length and gravitational acceleration.

For the new pendulum:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{4L}{g}} $

Simplifying this expression:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{4}\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2 \times 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

Thus, comparing the initial and new time periods:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2T_{\text{old}} $

The ratio of the new to the old time period is:

$ T_{\text{new}} : T_{\text{old}} = 2 : 1 $

Therefore, the ratio of the new and previous time periods is 2:1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The composition of gases in exosphere is
questions.solution:
The exosphere is primarily composed of light gases. Helium and hydrogen are the two main constituents found in this outermost layer of Earth's atmosphere.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A: Helium and Hydrogen.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A stone is thrown horizontally from the top of a 20 m high building with a speed of 12 m/s. It hits the ground at a distance R from the building. Taking g = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup> and neglecting air resistance will give :
questions.solution:
1. Vertical motion

  • Initial vertical velocity $u_y = 0$

  • Height $h = 20\,$m

  • Acceleration $g = 10\,$m/s²

  • Use $$h = \tfrac12\,g\,t^2$$

    so $$20 = \tfrac12\cdot10\cdot t^2 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad t^2 = 4 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad t = 2\ \text{s}$$

  1. Horizontal motion

  • Horizontal speed $v_x = 12\,$m/s (constant)

  • Range $R = v_x \,t = 12 \times 2 = 24\,$m

Answer: R = 24 m (Option C)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: All objects experience a buoyancy when they are immersed in a fluid. Buoyancy is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A block of mass 20 kg slides on a rough horizontal plane surface. Let the speed of the block at a particular instant is 10 m/s. It comes to rest after travelling a distance of 20 m. Which one of the following could be the magnitude of the frictional force?
questions.solution:
Let’s use the work–energy principle. The loss in kinetic energy equals the work done by friction:

Initial kinetic energy

$$K_i = \tfrac12 m v^2 = \tfrac12 \times 20\,\text{kg} \times (10\,\text{m/s})^2 = 1000\,\text{J}.$$

Work done by friction

$$W_{\rm fric} = f \times s,$$

where $f$ is the frictional force and $s=20$ m is the stopping distance.

Equate work to energy change

$$f \,s = K_i \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f = \frac{1000\,\text{J}}{20\,\text{m}} = 50\,\text{N}.$$

Answer: 50 N (Option 4).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding a current-carrying solenoid is not correct?
options: The magnetic field inside the solenoid is uniform.
The current-carrying solenoid behaves like a bar magnet.
The magnetic field inside the solenoid increases with increase in current.
If a soft iron bar is inserted inside the solenoid, the magnetic field remains the same.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Ramesh cannot see distinctly objects kept beyond 2 m. This defect can be corrected by using a lens of power
questions.solution:
Ramesh's condition is known as myopia or nearsightedness, where he can't see objects clearly beyond a certain distance, in this case, 2 meters. To correct myopia, concave lenses (which have negative focal lengths) are typically used, because they help to diverge light rays before they reach the eye, thus moving the focal point back onto the retina.

We need to find the power of the lens required to correct his vision. The formula for the lens power $ P $ in diopters (D) is given by:

$$ P = \frac{1}{f} $$

where $ f $ is the focal length in meters. For Ramesh, the far point is at 2 meters, meaning the lens should have its focal length $ f $ set to -2 meters (negative because it's a concave lens). Substituting the focal length into the formula, we get:

$$ P = \frac{1}{-2 \text{ m}} = -0.5 \text{ D} $$

Therefore, the correct lens power needed to correct Ramesh's vision is -0.5 diopters.

So, the correct option is:

Option B: -0.5 D

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following graphs correctly represents the current (I) - voltage (V) variation for a rectangular piece of a semiconductor wafer?
questions.solution: The current flowing through the semiconductor is not linearly proportional to the voltage. In forward bias the current increases by increasing the voltage in parabolic way.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
When a solid body is partially or completely immersed in a fluid, the fluid exerts an upward force on the body. The magnitude of the force is equal to
1.the mass of the body
2.the weight of the displaced fluid by the body
Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
The correct choice is 2 only.

By Archimedes’ principle, the buoyant force equals the weight of the fluid displaced:

$$F_{\rm b} = \rho_{\rm fluid}\,V_{\rm disp}\,g$$

where

• ρ₍fluid₎ is the fluid’s density

• V₍disp₎ is the volume of fluid displaced

• g is the acceleration due to gravity.

It is not equal to the mass of the body.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A system that does NOT allow exchange of heat with its surrounding is called
questions.solution:
Explanation:

Adiabatic Process:

An adiabatic process occurs in a thermodynamic system when there is no exchange of heat between the system and its surroundings. This means that throughout the adiabatic process, the system insulates itself against heat transfer.

Mathematically, this is represented by $ ΔQ = 0 $, indicating no heat transfer.

Additional Information:

Isothermal Process: In this process, the system undergoes changes such that its temperature remains constant.

Isobaric Process: This involves changes where the pressure remains constant.

Isochoric Process: Here, the system's volume remains constant during the change.

Isentropic Process:

In an isentropic process, the entropy of the system remains constant. It is also referred to as a constant-entropy process.

This process is both reversible and adiabatic, meaning no heat or matter is transferred.

It is an idealized concept in thermodynamics, often serving as a model to aspire to.

Entropy:

Entropy measures the amount of thermal energy in a system that cannot be used to perform work, per unit of temperature.

Over time, the entropy of the world tends to increase as systems move towards less equilibrium.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following wavelength radiations may correspond to an X-ray ?
questions.solution:
X-rays have very short wavelengths, usually in the range of about

$$ 0.01 \text{ nm to } 10 \text{ nm} $$

Now check the options:

  • $1 \text{ nm}$: lies in the X-ray range

  • $10 \text{ nm}$: at the extreme end, but usually not taken as the standard answer here

  • $100 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

  • $300 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{1 \text{ nm}} $$

Option 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following terms cannot represent electrical power in a circuit?
questions.solution:
Electrical power in a circuit can be expressed using different formulas based on the known quantities of voltage (V), current (I), and resistance (R). Let's analyze each option to determine which one is not a valid representation.

Option A: VI

This is a standard formula for electrical power, where power (P) is the product of voltage (V) and current (I):

$$ P = VI $$

Option B: I2 / R

This expression is incorrect. It resembles the formula for power, but power (P) is actually given by:

$$ P = I^2 R $$

The term I2 / R does not correctly represent electrical power.

Option C: I2R

This is another valid formula for electrical power, derived from Ohm's Law. Power (P) can be expressed as:

$$ P = I^2 R $$

Option D: V2 / R

This is also a correct formula for electrical power. By substituting Ohm's Law into the power formula, we get:

$$ P = \frac{V^2}{R} $$

So, the term that cannot represent electrical power is Option B: I2 / R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the lowest possible temperature?
questions.solution:
The lowest possible temperature among the given options is Option D: $-273$° Celsius. This temperature is equivalent to absolute zero, which is 0 Kelvin ($0 \, \text{K}$). Absolute zero is the theoretical temperature at which substances contain no thermal energy and particles are at their minimum motion. It marks the complete absence of heat energy.

In the Celsius scale, absolute zero is expressed as $-273.15$° Celsius. Thus, any temperature lower numerically like $-273$° Celsius, symbolically represents this concept of absolute zero. No temperature can be lower than absolute zero as it is the absolute limit of thermal motion in particle physics.

Therefore, in the context of the given options, $-273$° Celsius is the representation of the theoretical minimum temperature possible.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the usual unit of measurement for Air Pressure used in India?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Millibar. Here's why:

Air pressure is the weight of the atmosphere pressing down on a given area. It's measured as the force per unit area. Here's a breakdown of why the other options are incorrect and why millibars are used:

  • Centimeter (Option A): Centimeters measure length or distance, not pressure.
  • Milligram (Option C): Milligrams measure mass, not pressure.
  • Milliliter (Option D): Milliliters measure volume, not pressure.
  • Millibar (Option B): Millibars are the standard unit of measurement for air pressure in India and many other parts of the world. A millibar is a unit of pressure equal to 1/1000 of a bar.

In India, weather reports and forecasts typically use millibars (mb) to indicate air pressure. Other units of measurement for air pressure exist (like inches of mercury), but millibars are the most common in India.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following optical phenomena supports that the light is a transverse wave?
questions.solution:
The phenomenon that supports the idea that light is a transverse wave is Option D: Polarization.

To understand why polarization is indicative of light being a transverse wave, let's delve into the concept a bit more:

Light, as an electromagnetic wave, consists of oscillating electric and magnetic fields that are perpendicular to each other and to the direction of wave propagation. In the case of a transverse wave, the oscillations occur in a direction perpendicular to the direction of the wave's advance.

Polarization refers to the orientation of the oscillations of the light wave. When light passes through a polarizing filter, the filter only allows waves oscillating in a particular direction to pass through. This demonstrates that light waves have orientations perpendicular to their direction of travel, which is a key characteristic of transverse waves.

To summarize why the other options are not indicative of light being a transverse wave:

Option A: Refraction: A phenomenon where light changes direction when it passes from one medium to another due to a change in speed. This is related to the wave nature of light but does not specifically indicate that light is transverse.

Option B: Diffraction: The bending of light waves around obstacles and the spreading of light waves when they pass through small openings. While this is also a wave property, it is not specific to light being transverse.

Option C: Interference: This is the phenomenon where two or more light waves superpose to form a resultant wave. It demonstrates the wave nature of light but does not specifically indicate that light is a transverse wave.

Therefore, polarization is the phenomenon that confirms the transverse nature of light waves, as it directly shows that light has specific orientations of vibration perpendicular to the direction of propagation.

The mathematical representation for polarized light can be expressed if one considers the electric field vector $$\vec{E}$$ which oscillates in a specific plane, indicating transverse wave characteristics:

$$\vec{E}(z,t) = E_0 \cos(kz - \omega t + \phi) \hat{e}$$

where:

  • $$E_0$$ is the amplitude of the electric field
  • $$k$$ is the wave number
  • $$\omega$$ is the angular frequency
  • $$\phi$$ is the phase constant
  • $$\hat{e}$$ is the unit vector indicating the direction of polarization

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The work done in moving a charge of 2 coulomb (C) from point A to point B is 24 J. What is the potential difference between A and B?
questions.solution:
The potential difference between A and B is given by

$$ \Delta V \;=\;\frac{W}{q} $$

where

• $W = 24\text{ J}$ is the work done, and

• $q = 2\text{ C}$ is the charge.

Plugging in,

$$ \Delta V = \frac{24}{2} = 12\text{ V}. $$

So the correct choice is 12 V.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Refining of petroleum is carried out using which one of the following techniques?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one: of the following is considered to be the purest source of water ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 4: Rain water}$$

Reason:

Rain water is considered the purest natural source of water because it is formed by the evaporation of water from rivers, lakes, and seas. During evaporation, most impurities and salts are left behind.

So, among the given options, the purest source of water is:

$$\text{Rain water}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Hydrogenation of vegetable oils using nickel catalyst is an example of
questions.solution:
The hydrogenation of vegetable oils using a nickel catalyst is an example of an addition reaction.

In the hydrogenation process, hydrogen molecules ($H_2$) are added to the unsaturated carbon-carbon double bonds ($C=C$) in the fatty acid chains of vegetable oils. This addition of hydrogen atoms converts double bonds into single bonds, effectively saturating the oil and turning it into a solid or semi-solid form, like margarine or shortening.

The generalized equation for this reaction is:

$$ \text{RCH=CHR} + \text{H}_2 \xrightarrow{\text{Ni}} \text{RCH}_2\text{CH}_2\text{R} $$

Here, $ \text{RCH=CHR} $ represents an unsaturated fatty acid with a double bond, and $\text{Ni}$ is the nickel catalyst that facilitates the reaction by lowering the activation energy, enabling the hydrogen molecules to add across the double bond more efficiently.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following reactions is an example of decomposition reaction?
options: CH4 (g) + 2O2 (g) → CO2(g) + 2H2O(g)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is also known as Dry ice in its solid form?
questions.solution:
Dry ice is actually the solid form of a very common gas. The compound known as dry ice is carbon dioxide.

Its chemical formula is $$CO_2$$.

Looking at the options you gave:

Option 1: $$SiO_2$$ is Silicon dioxide, which is more commonly known as silica.

Option 2: $$CO_2$$ is Carbon dioxide.

Option 3: $$CaO$$ is Calcium oxide, or quicklime.

Option 4: $$MgO$$ is Magnesium oxide.

So, the one that is also known as dry ice in its solid form is $$CO_2$$, Carbon dioxide.

That means Option 2 is the correct one! Dry ice gets its name because it doesn't melt into a liquid; instead, it goes directly from a solid to a gas, a process called sublimation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding oxidation and reduction reactions is NOT correct?
options: If a substance loses hydrogen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance loses oxygen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance gains hydrogen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance gains oxygen during reaction, it is oxidized
questions.solution:
The statement that’s NOT correct is Option 1.

Here's why:

Oxidation involves either gaining O₂ or losing H₂ (loss of electrons).

Reduction involves either losing O₂ or gaining H₂ (gain of electrons).

So losing hydrogen is an oxidation process, not a reduction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: Half mole of nitrogen gas is measured 11·2 litre at STP
17 gram of ammonia gas contains 6·022 × 10²³ molecules at STP
22·4 litre of CO₂ gas at STP contains 44 gram of molecules
4 gram of hydrogen gas contains 6·022 × 10²³ molecules
questions.solution:
Let’s check each statement using the molar volume (1 mol gas at STP = 22.4 L) and molar masses:

½ mol N₂ → 0.5×22.4 L = 11.2 L ✅

17 g NH₃ (M = 17 g/mol) → 1 mol → 6.022×10²³ molecules ✅

22.4 L CO₂ → 1 mol → 44 g CO₂ ✅

4 g H₂ (M = 2 g/mol) →

$$n=\frac{4}{2}=2\;\text{mol}$$

$$N=2\times6.022\times10^{23}=1.2044\times10^{24}\;\text{molecules}$$

Statement says only 6.022×10²³ molecules, so it’s wrong. ❌

Answer: Option 4 is NOT correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following is the correct arrangement of halogens in the increasing order of their oxidizing nature?
questions.solution:
The correct arrangement of halogens in the increasing order of their oxidizing nature is:

Option D: I, Br, Cl, F

Explanation

The oxidizing power of halogens decreases as you move down the group in the periodic table. This is because the ability of the halogen atoms to gain an electron (to be reduced) decreases down the group due to the increasing size of the atoms and the increasing shielding effect which makes it harder for the nucleus to attract additional electrons.

Therefore, the correct order from least oxidizing to most oxidizing is:

Iodine (I)

Bromine (Br)

Chlorine (Cl)

Fluorine (F)

Fluorine is the strongest oxidizing agent among the halogens because it has the smallest atomic size and the highest electronegativity.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: What happens when the fat and oil containing food materials are left outside for a long time?
options: Fats and oils in the food get oxidized
Fats and oils in the food get reduced
Fats and oils in the food get ice-covered
questions.solution:
Option 1

When fats and oils in foods are exposed to air (especially light, heat or trace metals) over time, they undergo auto-oxidation, commonly called “rancidity.” Here’s what happens:

Initiation

• A hydrogen atom is abstracted from a fatty acid (usually at a double bond), forming a lipid radical (L•).

Propagation

• The lipid radical reacts with O₂ to give a peroxyl radical (LOO•).

• That peroxyl radical steals H from another fatty acid, creating a hydroperoxide (LOOH) and a new lipid radical—fueling a chain reaction.

Termination

• Two radicals combine to form non-radical products, but by then you’ve already formed volatile aldehydes, ketones and acids responsible for off-flavors and odors.

Why the other options don’t fit

• Reduction (Option 2) would require a hydrogen‐rich environment or specific enzymes—unlikely in food left out.

• Ice formation (Option 3) only occurs below 0 °C.

• “No reaction” (Option 4) isn’t true—oxidative rancidity happens at room temperature given time and oxygen.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: The interconversion of states of matter is a physical change
During burning of a candle, both chemical and physical changes take place
During a physical change, the chemical composition of miolecules changes
Rusting of iron is a chemical change
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

Option 3: During a physical change, the chemical composition of molecules changes.

Explanation:

In a physical change, only the shape, size, or state of a substance may change. The chemical composition remains the same.

Let us check the other options:

  1. The interconversion of states of matter is a physical change

    This is correct.

    Example: ice changing into water, water changing into steam.

  2. During burning of a candle, both chemical and physical changes take place

    This is correct.

  • Melting of wax is a physical change

  • Burning of wax is a chemical change

  1. Rusting of iron is a chemical change

    This is correct because a new substance, iron oxide, is formed.

Final Answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 3}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The process whereby certain minerals absorb water, expand and change is called as
questions.solution:
The process whereby certain minerals absorb water, expand, and undergo a chemical change is called Hydration.

In geological contexts, hydration is often observed when minerals like anhydrite transform into gypsum. This process involves the incorporation of water molecules into the mineral structure, leading to an increase in volume and a change in the chemical composition. Hydration is distinct from other weathering processes like oxidation, hydrolysis, and carbonation, each of which involves different chemical reactions and agents.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following oxides has the highest melting point?
questions.solution:
The oxides presented in the options are sodium oxide (Na$_2$O), magnesium oxide (MgO), iron(III) oxide (Fe$_2$O$_3$), and copper(II) oxide (CuO). To determine which one has the highest melting point, we need to consider the nature of the chemical bonding and lattice structure of each oxide.

1. Na$_2$O (Sodium Oxide): This is an ionic compound formed between sodium and oxygen and has ionic bonds. The melting point of Na$_2$O is relatively high but not as high as some other oxides on the list.

2. MgO (Magnesium Oxide): This is also an ionic compound but with a stronger ionic bond compared to Na$_2$O due to the higher charge density of Mg$^{2+}$ as opposed to Na$^{+}$. MgO has a very high melting point due to the strong electrostatic forces of attraction between Mg$^{2+}$ and O$^{2-}$ ions.

3. Fe$_2$O$_3$ (Iron(III) Oxide): This is an oxide of iron where iron is in the +3 oxidation state. Fe$_2$O$_3$ is composed of both ionic and some covalent character. Its melting point is also high but typically not as high as that of MgO.

4. CuO (Copper(II) Oxide): Copper(II) oxide has a partially covalent and partially ionic bond structure. Its melting point is high but lower compared to that of MgO and Fe$_2$O$_3$.

Among the given options, MgO (Magnesium Oxide) has the highest melting point due to its strong ionic bonds between Mg$^{2+}$ and O$^{2-}$ ions. The strong electrostatic forces result in a very high melting point, which is significantly higher than that of the other listed oxides.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option B: MgO.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following is NOT true with respect to colloidal solution?
options: Particles are uniformly distributed throughout the solution
Colloidal solution is homogenous in nature
They do not settle down when kept undisturbed
questions.solution:
To determine which option is not true with respect to colloidal solutions, let's examine each statement in detail:

Option A: Particles are uniformly distributed throughout the solution

This statement is true. In a colloidal solution, the particles, known as the dispersed phase, are distributed uniformly throughout the dispersing medium. This uniform distribution is a key characteristic of colloidal solutions.

Option B: Colloidal solution is homogenous in nature

This statement is not true. Although colloidal solutions appear homogeneous to the naked eye, they are actually heterogeneous in nature. The dispersed particles are microscopically dispersed throughout the medium, which causes deviations from true homogeneity when observed under a microscope.

Option C: They show Tyndall effect

This statement is true. Colloidal solutions exhibit the Tyndall effect, which is the scattering of light by colloidal particles. This scattering makes a beam of light visible when it passes through the colloidal solution.

Option D: They do not settle down when kept undisturbed

This statement is true. In a colloidal solution, the particles are small enough that they do not settle down due to gravity. Instead, they remain in a dispersed state, preventing sedimentation.

Based on the above analysis, the statement that is NOT true with respect to colloidal solutions is:

Option B: Colloidal solution is homogenous in nature

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
In the following redox reaction, which compound is acting as a reducing agent ?

N2H4 + 2H2O2 → N2 + 4H2O
questions.solution:
To find the reducing agent, we check which substance gets oxidised.

Given reaction:

$$ N_2H_4 + 2H_2O_2 \rightarrow N_2 + 4H_2O $$

Step 1: Find oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2H_4$

Let oxidation state of nitrogen be $x$.

In $N_2H_4$:

  • Hydrogen has oxidation state $+1$

So,

$$ 2x + 4(+1) = 0 $$

$$ 2x + 4 = 0 $$

$$ 2x = -4 $$

$$ x = -2 $$

So, oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2H_4$ is $-2$.

Step 2: Find oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2$

In elemental form,

$$ \text{Oxidation state of } N \text{ in } N_2 = 0 $$

So nitrogen changes from:

$$ -2 \rightarrow 0 $$

This is oxidation.

Step 3: Identify reducing agent

The substance which gets oxidised acts as the reducing agent.

Here, $N_2H_4$ is oxidised to $N_2$.

Therefore, the reducing agent is:

$$ \boxed{N_2H_4} $$

Correct option: 2. $N_2H_4$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Common salt (NaCl) is not used as a raw material for preparation of which one of the following compounds?
questions.solution:
Common salt (NaCl) is not used as a raw material for the preparation of Plaster of Paris.

Plaster of Paris is chemically calcium sulfate hemihydrate, with the formula $$ \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} $$, and is produced from gypsum ($\text{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$) through a heating process.

Here's a quick explanation of whether and how NaCl is involved in the production of the other compounds:

Bleaching Powder: It is prepared using calcium hydroxide ($$\text{Ca(OH)}_2$$) and chlorine gas, which can be derived from the electrolysis of NaCl solution.

Baking Soda: Sodium bicarbonate ($$\text{NaHCO}_3$$) is made using the Solvay process, which involves NaCl, ammonia, and carbon dioxide.

Washing Soda: Sodium carbonate ($$\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$$) is also produced via the Solvay process, using NaCl as an essential raw material.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option C: Plaster of Paris.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Which one among the following has valency of 3?
questions.solution:
Valency is the number of electrons an atom needs to gain, lose or share to complete its valence shell. For the given elements:

Carbon

– Electron configuration: $$1s^2\,2s^2\,2p^2$$

– Valence electrons: 4 → Valency = 4

Oxygen

– Electron configuration: $$1s^2\,2s^2\,2p^4$$

– Valence electrons: 6 → Valency = 2

Nitrogen

– Electron configuration: $$1s^2\,2s^2\,2p^3$$

– Valence electrons: 5 → Needs 3 more → Valency = 3

Sodium

– Electron configuration: $$1s^2\,2s^2\,2p^6\,3s^1$$

– Valence electron: 1 → Valency = 1

Answer: Nitrogen (Option 3) has a valency of 3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT a chemical change ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 3: Melting of ice.

Reason:

A chemical change is a change in which a new substance is formed.

Let us check each option:

  1. Burning of coal

    Coal burns and forms new substances like carbon dioxide, ash, etc.

    So, it is a chemical change.

  2. Rusting of metal

    Iron reacts with oxygen and moisture to form rust.

    Rust is a new substance.

    So, it is a chemical change.

  3. Melting of ice

    Ice changes into water only. No new substance is formed.

    It is only a physical change.

    So, this is not a chemical change.

  4. Reaction of acid with base

    Acid and base react to form salt and water.

    New substances are formed.

    So, it is a chemical change.

Final Answer:

Option 3: Melting of ice

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Consider the following reaction:

2HgO $ \stackrel{\Delta}{\longrightarrow} $ 2Hg + O2

The respective state of HgO, Hg and O2 in the above reaction is
questions.solution:
The reaction in question is the thermal decomposition of mercury(II) oxide (HgO) into elemental mercury (Hg) and oxygen gas (O2). The respective states of each of these substances are as follows:

Mercury(II) oxide (HgO) is a solid at room temperature. When it decomposes upon heating, it forms elemental mercury, which is a liquid at room temperature, and oxygen gas.

Thus, the correct option is:

Option D

Solid, liquid, gas

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about dihydrogen (H2) is not correct?
options: H<sub>2</sub> is lighter than air and insoluble in water.
H<sub>2</sub> is inert at room temperature due to high H-H bond dissociation enthalpy.
H<sub>2</sub> reacts with alkali metals at high temperature to yield metal hydrides.
A mixture of NO<sub>2</sub> and H<sub>2</sub> is known as Syngas.
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement regarding dihydrogen (H₂):

Option A: "H₂ is lighter than air and insoluble in water."

Correct. Hydrogen is indeed lighter than air and has very low solubility in water.

Option B: "H₂ is inert at room temperature due to high H-H bond dissociation enthalpy."

Correct. The H-H bond in H₂ is very strong (with a high dissociation enthalpy, approximately 436 kJ/mol), which makes the molecule relatively unreactive (or inert) under standard conditions unless a catalyst or extreme conditions are provided.

Option C: "H₂ reacts with alkali metals at high temperature to yield metal hydrides."

Correct. Alkali metals can react with hydrogen gas at elevated temperatures to form corresponding metal hydrides (e.g., sodium hydride, NaH).

Option D: "A mixture of NO₂ and H₂ is known as Syngas."

Not Correct. Syngas, short for synthesis gas, is typically a mixture of carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H₂). A mixture of nitrogen dioxide (NO₂) and H₂ does not constitute syngas.

Therefore, the statement that is not correct is Option D.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following nitrogen oxides may dimerize?
questions.solution:
The oxide that dimerizes is NO₂. Here’s why:

NO₂ is a free radical (it has an unpaired electron).

Two NO₂ molecules can pair up that unpaired electron to form dinitrogen tetroxide, N₂O₄.

The equilibrium is:

$$2\,NO_2 \;\rightleftharpoons\; N_2O_4$$

At lower temperatures the equilibrium shifts toward N₂O₄ (the dimer), while at higher temperatures it shifts back to NO₂.

None of the other oxides listed (N₂O, N₂O₃, N₂O₅) form such dimers under normal conditions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: Water of crystallization is the fixed number of water molecules present in one formula unit of a salt
The molecular formula of Plaster of Paris is CaSO<sub>4</sub>:2H<sub>2</sub>O
Washing soda is used for removing the permanent hardness of water
The atmosphere of Venus is made up of thick white and yellow clouds of sulphuric acid
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

$$\text{Option 2}$$

Reason:

Plaster of Paris has the formula:

$$\text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \dfrac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O}$$

It is also written as:

$$2\text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \text{H}_2\text{O}$$

But in Option 2, it is given as:

$$\text{CaSO}_4 : 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$$

This is wrong.

Checking the other options:

  1. Water of crystallization is the fixed number of water molecules present in one formula unit of a salt — correct.

  2. Plaster of Paris formula given is wrong — not correct.

  3. Washing soda is used for removing permanent hardness of water — correct.

  4. Venus has thick white and yellow clouds of sulphuric acid — correct.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 2}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is correct?
options: Pyroxene is commonly found in meteorites
Amphiboles form about 20% of the Earth's crust
The word 'sedimentary' is derived from the Latin word kelimentum
questions.solution:
The correct statement is Option 1: Pyroxene is commonly found in meteorites.

• Many stony (chondritic) meteorites are dominated by silicate minerals—primarily olivine and pyroxene.

• Amphiboles make up only a few percent (not ~20%) of the Earth’s crust minerals.

• Quartz is essentially insoluble in pure water.

• “Sedimentary” comes from the Latin sedimentum (“a settling”), not “kelimentum.”

questions.questionText: As proposed by Dalton, which of the following symbol represents phosphorus?
questions.solution:

    • Dalton, the father of modern atomic theory, was the first to use symbols to represent elements.
    • Dalton's symbols were generally circles with different symbols, such as a dot for hydrogen, a cross for sulfur, or circles with letters like C for copper and L for lead.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: The molecules of soap are sodium or potassium salts of long chain fatty acids
The molecules of soap contain both hydrophobic and hydrophilic ends
Detergents are more effective than soaps in hard water
In micelles the ionic-end of the molecules is towards oil droplet while the other end faces outside
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 4.

Reason: In a micelle formed in water, the ionic (hydrophilic) ends face the surrounding aqueous medium, while the non-polar (hydrophobic) tails point inward toward the trapped oil or grease. Option 4 has this orientation reversed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Water gas is a mixture of:
questions.solution:
Water gas is a mixture of carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H₂). It is produced by passing steam over hot carbon (coke) at high temperatures, typically around 1000°C. The chemical reaction for the formation of water gas is as follows:

$$\text{C} + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{CO} + \text{H}_2$$

Given this information, the correct option is:

Option B: CO and H₂
